text: Adolescence isn’t just about hormones; it’s a brain makeover. During these years, your brain is pruning away extra connections, like trimming a tree. This process, known as synaptic pruning, boosts efficiency and speeds up thinking. Studies show that by late adolescence, the brain has cut away up to 50% of its connections. It’s not just chaos; it’s a strategic upgrade for adulthood. Want to understand more about your brain? Like and follow for deeper dives into neuroscience!
